guide.title: Use control detail to keep ownership, evidence, workflow, and requirement coverage together
guide.summary: The catalog list is for browsing. The selected control workspace is where mappings and supporting material are maintained.
body: Create internal controls that describe the safeguard or expected practice you want to govern.
Link framework requirements to each control when you need to show coverage against a standard or regulation.
Keep owners, workflow state, evidence summary, and artifacts current inside the control detail workspace.

concepts.label: Control
concepts.summary: A safeguard, requirement, or expected practice that should exist and be reviewed.
concepts.why_it_exists: Controls express what good governance or protection should look like. They become a central checkpoint for evidence and review.
how_to_use: Create controls for the safeguards your organization wants to track.
Review them regularly and attach evidence when needed.

relations.summary: Controls help protect or govern assets.
Controls are one of the main ways to reduce risk.
Controls can be aligned with frameworks and requirements.

concepts.label: Control Framework
concepts.summary: A named framework or standard that groups one set of control requirements.
concepts.why_it_exists: Frameworks help the organization explain where a control expectation comes from.
how_to_use: Register the framework first, then add requirements under it.
Map your internal controls to those requirements when you need traceability.

concepts.label: Control Requirement
concepts.summary: One requirement or clause inside a framework.
concepts.why_it_exists: Requirements create the traceable link between internal controls and external obligations or standards.
how_to_use: Add requirements under the framework they belong to.
Map internal controls when you need to show coverage.

relations.summary: A requirement is part of one framework.
Internal controls can cover one or more requirements.

concepts.label: Control Coverage Mapping
concepts.summary: The explicit relationship between one internal control and one framework requirement, including the stated coverage level.
concepts.why_it_exists: Coverage mapping makes framework adoption and audit traceability concrete instead of implied.
how_to_use: Link the requirement to the control and choose the coverage level that best reflects reality.
Add a short note when the relationship needs qualification or context.

relations.summary: The mapping belongs to one internal control.
The mapping points to one framework requirement.
